title: Artificer's Armory
description: (Beta) A weapon expansion for mage and spellcaster playstyles.
body:
_Requires GeckoLib_

This mod introduces several new magical weapons and tools designed for mage or spellcaster playstyles while attempting to be somewhat vanilla friendly. This mod is also meant to be complimentary to the [Iron's Spells](https://iron.wiki/) and [Better Combat](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/better-combat-by-daedelus) mods, but can be used without them. Each weapon has its own unique playstyle and abilities, and is designed to be easy to pick up and understand, and rewarding to master, with a plethora of unique enchantments that affect how they function. **This mod is currently incomplete, and considered to be in beta. The full version of this mod will have at least 10 unique weapons. Please feel free to leave feedback or suggestions in the issues page on Github or in [my discord](https://discord.gg/ehTCanCgXa).** --- # Weapons See the [wiki](https://github.com/TrashElemental/Artificers-Armory/wiki) for more detailed information on weapons and their unique enchantments. ## Tuning Fork ![The recipe pattern for the tuning fork.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/HwSC3CR4/images/b7200112b316a827f585436e7cc08edc0850a640.png) A resonating tuning fork that can be used as a melee or area of effect weapon that can create shockwaves of sound when it strikes foes. Its primary use, however, is to apply a temporary enchantment to your other gear called 'Resonance'. Resonant armors will have increased damage negation, resonant tools can break extra blocks, resonant weapons will cause delayed shockwaves when they strike foes, and resonant bows and crossbows can fire a second shot after a short delay. The tuning fork can be further enchanted to increase the frequency, power, or effectiveness of your shockwaves and the resonance enchantment. ## Wrench ![The recipe pattern for the wrench.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/HwSC3CR4/images/1962c54cbf91dac92fc2d65754323751c497a592.png) An artificer's wrench that can be used as a melee weapon, or to construct sentries that will autonomously attack nearby enemies. By default, you can construct two types of sentries, a basic sentry that will shoot arrows at nearby enemies, and a hover sentry that will fly over your shoulder and attack when you attack. By interacting with a sentry using a wrench, you can 'overclock' the sentry, causing it to attack faster, but also shortening its lifespan. By enchanting the wrench, you can construct additional sentry types like area-denial electro coils, ridable mobile gears, or long range laser arrays. The type of sentry you create using the wrench can be cycled by shift-using the item. Permanent sentries can also be created by using the wrench on one of three blocks placed on top of an Iron Bars block. Use a dispenser to create a basic sentry, a lightning rod to create an electro coil, or a redstone block to create a redstone laser array. ![Sentries!](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/HwSC3CR4/images/29da2e5312a1be1b0ce7213ddf2ad33bc22c56d6.png) ## Sprig ![The recipe pattern for the sprig.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/HwSC3CR4/images/1a28ec5f8c7ffb10ccf3277fc70a7a281a7ea40d.png) The Sprig is a druidic staff used to fire a beam of concentrated nature magic. This weapon excels at area control and shaping the battlefield, as well as unlocking new movement options for its wielder. By firing normally, you can grow a line of bushes along the ground that will slow and damage enemies. By crouching while you fire, you will create a Sprout that can be climbed to reach new heights. This weapon is also a stout shilellagh, capable of delivering a hearty whack. Enchant it further to unlock the full potential of nature, create goodberries that can be eaten to restore health and hunger, create a small army of mandrakes, or recover your energy in the sunlight. The Sprig's full potential can be unlocked by firing its beam at natural blocks like flowers, leaves, or logs. ## Oculus ![The recipe pattern for qthe oculus.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/HwSC3CR4/images/fd7b25a2e2b23ecf71298ddbde2a87131a045821.png) The Oculus is a ponderous orb used to create volleys of magical homing projectiles. By channeling the item, you can create up to three magical projectiles that will orbit you until you release the oculus, at which point they will become able to chase down and deal substantial damage to enemies. By further enchanting the oculus, you can cause it to create black holes, control the projectiles with your mind, or create defensive fields. ## Firebrand ![The recipe pattern for the firebrand.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/599fdcc0104ffe35f0398a1b5d961d6dd3240877.png) The Firebrand is a weaponized torch enchanted with a perpetual flame that can be used to cast fireballs or light up your surroundings. While held in either hand, it will shed light like a torch at your location, and can also be used to break certain blocks like cobwebs and ice very quickly. Melee attacks with this weapon will ignite enemies, but it shines the brightest as a ranged weapon capable of shooting fireballs. The firebrand can be further enchanted to become a flamethrower, a healing beacon, a charged blaster, or capable of burning even fireproof enemies. ## Censer ![The recipe pattern for the censer.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c3a3467207972c662ef5fdea6e5acbdd3f729bf5.png) A blessed flail with a censer as its head. A melee weapon which can also be used to disperse clouds of incense that will apply an effect to nearby entities. By default, this effect is 'Blessing,' a minor boost to several attributes. However, by using the censer with a potion in your offhand, you can change the stored effect the incense will apply. You can use a milk bucket to reset the effect to Blessing. Positive effects will only affect you and your allies, while negative effects will ignore you and your allies. The censer can be further enchanted to transmute positive effects into negative ones for your enemies (and vice versa for your allies), affect a wider area, or heal your allies with the incense while amaging your enemies. ## Blight ![The recipe pattern for the blight.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b3332f632ba2309a3ea99af71e3c3aedb4393439.png) A plague doctor's cane enchanted with a lingering hex that allows its wielder to exhale a cloud of noxious breath. A melee weapon that can be used for up to four seconds to blast a cone in front of you with a toxic cloud, dealing damage and inflicting the Plague effect, which debilitates and slowly damages enemies. By further enchanting the blight, you can cause the plague effect to create rats that seek out uninfected targets and spread the plague, cause riots among enemies with delirium, or cause chain reactions of black death when enemies die. ## Spirit Candle ![The recipe pattern for the Spirit Candle.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d20ed75b9ebcf8c0db441f70782159d108972a09.png) Crafted from bone ash and compacted grave wax, the spirit candle enables a necromancer to conjure undead servants from the ethereal plane, so long as they **maintain their concentration by holding the candle in either hand.** Your undead servants will proactively target your enemies, and try to spread out their aggression to take on crowds. By using the candle, you can continually channel it to empower your minions, slowly healing them and boosting their offensive abilities. If you want all your minions to focus on one target, you can crouch-use the item while looking at a target to mark it. The spirit candle can be further enchanted to provide a stronger buff to your minions, summon skeletal priests that bless them, or even sacrifice all your minions to avoid your own death. ## Chime ![The recipe pattern for the chime.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c7baf987b518766c47a9c03c82f912e03af3fa0f.png) A hand bell that can be rung to get the attention of a familiar spirit that takes the form of a golden allay. Your familiar is pretty handy, and has a variety of abilities it can use in and out of combat, such as protecting you from falling or fire, highlighting nearby monsters, or even alerting you to hidden loot. Your familiar will also pick up and carry items for you, which can be toggled to either deposit held items in nearby containers, or ignore them, by interacting with the familiar using a chime. You can dismiss your familiar by crouch-using the chime on them. Unlike the Spirit Candle, you do **not** need to keep the chime in your hand, but it can still be used to highlight an item for your familiar to retrieve or an enemy to target. While your familiar is active, a small icon of the familiar's face and a heart will appear to the right of your hotbar. The heart will indicate the familiar's health, and the face will indicate when the familiar is ready to use one of its 'triggered' abilities, such as catching you from a fall or putting you out when you are on fire. Your familiar can be further enhanced by the chime's unique enchantments, allowing it to specialize into a combat role from healer, bruiser, protector, or prankster. ![A lineup of the base familiar and its subclasses.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/165e31e634a64b3148645ccf4eca56dcced6bfc3.png) By giving the Chime used to summon a familiar certain names in an anvil, you can cause the familiar to take on a special appearance... ![Costumes your familiar can wear through special names given to the chime used to summon it.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6e3133b6892f76134397a40c5edf36d1f1aa23d9.png) ## Future Weapon Plans The full version of this mod is expected to have at least 10 weapon types. The following are planned: * Deck Of Cards - A magical deck of cards that can be placed on the ground or thrown, with a different effect for each suit. * Voodoo Pin - A cursed pin that can be used to cause mischief among your enemies. ## Credits * The Scarecrow costume for the Familiar references the [Tameable Beasts](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/tameable-beasts) mod by [themodderg](https://www.curseforge.com/members/themodderg/projects). * The Mage costume for the familiar references the [Companions!](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/companions-mod) mod by [xylonity](https://www.curseforge.com/members/xylonity/projects). * The 'Calling Card' and 'Amethyst Candy' textures are modified textures purchased from [Glionox](https://glionox.itch.io/items16). * The 'Mandrake Salad' and 'Mandrake Root' textures are modified textures purchased from [PixelTier](https://pixeltier.itch.io/)
